- Celebrities Who Have Been Convicted Of Tax Fraud: Kind: captions Language: en Reality stars and rappers. Actors and artists. For one reason or another these celebrities avoided paying the big bucks. Reality stars seem particularly susceptible to tax troubles, so we're kicking it off with "Jersey Shore" star Mike Sorrentino, better known as "The Situation." The Staten Island-born star rose to fame in 2009 with the debut of the addictive MTV reality series, which ran until 2012. Two years after the show ended, Mike was indicted on charges that he had avoided paying taxes on $8.9 million in income. Per Forbes, both Mike and his brother, Marc Sorrentino, were accused of filing false tax returns and committing fraud. They were also alleged to have used their companies to evade taxation, using company money for personal expenses like expensive cars and clothing, claiming them as legitimate business expenses. In January 2018, Mike and his brother pleaded guilty to the charges, and he was sentenced to eight months in federal prison. A few months later, he shared his thoughts on his approaching jail time with Us Weekly. He explained, Mike started his sentence a year after pleading guilty and was freed eight months later. Aside from the jail time, the reality star was also sentenced to two years of supervised release, a $10,000 fine, and 500 hours of community service. "Is prison like it is in 'Goodfellas'?" "Oh, um…" Long before he was involved in the infamous Fyre Festival debacle, Ja Rule got himself involved in a different kind of scandal — one of the tax fraud variety. In March 2011, the rapper pleaded guilty to three counts of failing to file a tax return and agreed to pay over $1.1 million in tax debts, plus added penalties. Prior to his sentencing, the star suggested that he had already paid millions in taxes and had simply fallen short. He confessed on "MTV's RapFix Live", stating, He was sentenced to 28 months in prison for the offense but actually served more time. That period was tacked on to a two-year sentence he was already facing for a 2007 weapons charge. He was eventually released from prison two months earlier than his anticipated July 2013 release. Sadly, Rule's tax trouble didn't end there. In 2021, the rapper and his wife, Aisha Atkins, were accused of refusing to pay more than $3 million in federal taxes, according to Radar. The money in question was reportedly owed from the years 2005 through 2010 and 2012 through 2017. Naturally, one of Rule's most famous foes, 50 Cent, took the opportunity to poke fun at the situation on Instagram. Sharing a screenshot of the Radar report and with laughing emojis in the caption, he wrote, In the '90s, Wesley Snipes found massive success in Hollywood with films like "White Men Can't Jump," "Demolition Man," and "Rising Sun." Unfortunately, the actor seemingly didn't file taxes after 1998. Per Fox News, the government claimed he made around $13.8 million during that time and racked up at least $2.7 million in back taxes. Per People, in 2008, the actor was convicted of three counts of willfully failing to file tax returns and was handed a three-year sentence. He was also fined $5 million for failing to file taxes, though at that time he was acquitted of five additional charges that included tax fraud and conspiracy. In 2010, an appeal he'd submitted against the charges was denied and he entered federal prison that December. During an appearance on "Larry King Live" that same year, Snipes suggested he felt his case had been mistreated by the media. "They claimed that, uh, there was a certain number of, uh, that was owed and that number has slid all over the place." He added, He ended up doing a little over two years of that sentence and was released in April 2013. Seven years later, he told The Guardian that his experience in jail gave him a new appreciation for spending time with loved ones. In December 2012, the youngest brother of the Baldwin acting dynasty, Stephen Baldwin, was arrested and charged with failing to pay his taxes. He pleaded not guilty at the time of his arrest. The actor was alleged to have amassed a $350,000 debt after neglecting to pay New York state taxes from 2008 to 2010. Per CNN, three months later, the actor pleaded guilty and confessed to having failed to file his taxes for three years. However, in a statement given while leaving court, he attempted to provide some explanation for his wrongdoings, saying, The actor added that he was excited to get his debt paid off. Surprisingly, although he faced up to four years in prison, Baldwin's plea deal didn't require any jail time. Instead, he had to pay $300,000 in back taxes, dodging the interest and penalty fees that would have made him liable for $400,000. The actor paid off the last of his debts in April 2014, a month before he found himself in trouble with the law again – this time for driving with a suspended license. In June 2012, singer Lauryn Hill — best known for her work with hip-hop group the Fugees before going solo — was charged with failing to file over three years of income taxes. Per MTV, the IRS estimated she'd earned more than $1.6 million within that period. The following month, the musician pleaded guilty to three counts of failing to file tax returns. Hill was sentenced in May 2013, and she was very vocal with the judge about her case. Per CNN, the star pointed out the amount of money her music had made for other people and insisted that she lived modestly by comparison. She stated, The singer famously addressed her legal troubles in a lengthy tirade on Tumblr, in which she continued to plead her case about living a humble lifestyle. Hill added that she made a conscious decision to step away from the mainstream after experiencing a crisis that made her feel devalued by the industry and belittled by the media. She noted, She was given a three-month prison stint alongside three months of house arrest. Additionally, she was asked to pay what she owed on top of a $60,000 fine. The singer served her time and was released a few days early in October 2013. Most people are familiar with media mogul Martha Stewart's legal troubles involving insider trading in 2004, but the icon has also found herself in some tax trouble, too. This time, it involved a complicated case where Stewart owed taxes to the state of New York. According to The New Yorker, the star's main residence — and site of many a photo shoot in her Martha Stewart Living magazine — happened to be in Westport, Connecticut. However, she also owned two properties in New York in the '90s — a cottage in East Hampton and an apartment in Manhattan. Stewart didn't deny owning property in the state in 1991 and 1992. Instead, she claimed that neither was a permanent residence and that both dwellings were undergoing renovations that rendered them uninhabitable. When Stewart was eventually tried for tax evasion years later in 1998, she was unable to deny residency due to some surprising pieces of evidence. For starters, the jacket copy for her books published in those years read, Further damning were photographs of both properties in her own magazine that satisfied the judge as to them being fully furnished, habitable, and lived in. Per The New York Times, it was eventually ruled that she pay back over $200,000, plus penalties and interest. Stewart reportedly paid that figure back in full. Country legend, Willie Nelson, got himself into so much trouble with the IRS that he had to put out an entire album to help repay the debt! As reported by The New York Times, back in 1990, the free-spirited musician was found to have accrued a tax debt estimated to be around $32 million — the largest ever owed by an individual at that time. He subsequently had multiple properties and belongings like touring equipment, master tapes, and gold and platinum records seized by federal agents, according to Rolling Stone. "Obviously I have to start off by letting you know, I'm not a cop." "I'm not either." "Okay, good." After his assets had been taken into possession, the IRS charged him a debt of $16.7 million. By 1993 that figure was brought down to $9 million, and after paying $3 million of that debt off, he decided to put out a two-disc collection to help pay the rest off. "The IRS Tapes: Who'll Buy My Memories" was released in 1992 and was pieced together from 35 years worth of seized recordings that the IRS gave him access to. In 1995, Nelson reflected on the experience with surprisingly fond memories. He told Rolling Stone, Power vocalist Toni Braxton made it big in the R&B world in the '90s with songs like "Un-Break My Heart" and "Breathe Again." Sadly, over the years she has found herself in trouble with the IRS not once, but twice. In 2018, The Blast reported that the star owed over $700,000 to the IRS and the state of California after four tax liens were filed against her. A year later, she was again revealed to owe a massive amount of back taxes with E! News reporting that the IRS had filed a lien against the star for over $340,000. Meanwhile, the state of California had filed its own tax lien against Braxton for $116,000. The almost half-a-million dollars that Braxton reportedly owed were dated back to 2017, which the outlet noted was a financially successful year for the singer. The performer had finished out her The Hits Tour while starring in the reality series, "Braxton Family Values." As of this video, it's unclear how much of her debts she has currently paid off. Sadly, financial issues are nothing new for Braxton, as the star has filed for bankruptcy twice in her life — first in 1998 and again in 2010. Two years after her second bankruptcy filing, she told "20/20" that while she was prone to overindulgence, the nature of her early record contracts also left her with little to live off of. "A hundred and seventy million in sales and you got $1,972? Is that right?" "I did. That's right. That's true." Fat Joe began his rise to fame in the '90s, making a name for himself in the rap scene. Unfortunately, by 2012, he'd started making headlines for something other than hit songs when he pleaded guilty to tax evasion. Per The Hollywood Reporter, the musician had reportedly made almost $3 million in just two years and was found to subsequently owe over $700,000 in unpaid taxes to the federal government. At the time, his defense attorney, Jeffrey Lichtman, told the New York Post, Six months later, the rapper was sentenced to four months in prison for tax evasion and served time between August and November 2013, per Rolling Stone. Six years later, he reflected on how his penalty made him reevaluate his prior beefs, telling "The Breakfast Club," "Like, I knew when I came out of jail I had to squash my s--- with JAY-Z. Squash my s--- with 50 Cent and any loose ends I had out there."

- Celebrities Facing PRISON for Tax Evasion: Kind: captions Language: en Celebrities Facing PRISON for Tax Evasion You know the saying, “Nothing is certain except death and taxes.” In this video, we’ll show you which celebrities neglected to report MILLIONS worth of income on their tax returns, and which ones didn’t even bother filing their taxes. Join us as we take a look at which celebrities are currently facing jail time for tax evasion, and who has already served their time in prison. You may be surprised who you see in this video. Let’s get started! Shakira The Colombian pop singer’s hips don’t lie, but it appears her numbers do. She was first accused of tax evasion in 2018. For the years between 2012 and 2014, she claimed to be residing in the Bahamas for tax purposes, but according to the prosecution, she was actually living in Spain! In 2019, Shakira testified in front of a judge that she was innocent. According to her agency, once she was informed of her tax obligation, she immediately paid what she owed, plus interest. Her appeal has been rejected by the court and a trial was ordered. She’s made headlines more recently because the three-year investigation has found that there is “sufficient evidence of criminality” for the case to proceed to trial. She’s currently facing six charges alleging she neglected to pay the Spanish government nearly $15 million in taxes between the years in question. Prosecutors said they are also seeking a $24.5 million fine. Shakira turned down a plea bargain offered by prosecutors, despite the fact that a trial date has not yet been set. If convicted and found guilty, prosecutors are asking the court to sentence her to eight years and two months in prison. Her case hinges on where she lived during the years in which she claimed to be a resident of the Bahamas. Let’s wish her the best. Wesley Snipes The actor narrowly avoided being charged with tax fraud in 2008 after a lengthy trial in which he made excuse after excuse. He sought every possible excuse to avoid paying taxes. He blamed his advisors for giving him false information. He claimed he was a nonresident alien, and he even stated that the IRS was an illegitimate government agency. His excuses were not enough to avoid a conviction. The actor was found guilty of three misdemeanor charges for failing to file his federal taxes 5 years in a row between 1999 and 2004. Snipes offered to pay $842,000 to help clear his $23.5 million debt, but instead he was sentenced to three years in prison and fined $5 million for willfully failing to file nearly $40 million worth of past tax returns. He served his time in federal prison between 2010 and 2013. Martha Stewart The American entrepreneur and domestic lifestyle innovator failed to pay taxes on her New York home to the sum of $220,000, claiming she shouldn't have to pay taxes on it since she didn't spend much time there. However, it isn't just about her New York home. As she was being questioned by federal authorities, Stewart was found guilty of lying to them. She said that she had an agreement with ImClone Systems to sell her shares of stock if the price ever dropped below $60, but it appears there was never any such deal. The company develops cancer-therapy drugs and happens to be run by her friend. Stewart ended up selling 4,000 shares worth $228,000 the day before the company announced their drug was rejected by the FDA and their stock tanked. Coincidence? Or was this insider trading? The feds couldn’t prove it was insider trading. However, they did prove she lied during the investigation. In March 2004, she was convicted of felony charges of conspiracy, obstruction of justice, and lying to federal investigators. She spent 5 months in jail, followed by 5 months of home confinement on her 153-acre estate. She was also ordered to pay $220,000 in back Toni Braxton 2019 was definitely not Toni’s best year. Just after New Years she called off her wedding to rapper Birdman and the pair split. A few days later, it came to light that she owed nearly half a million dollars in delinquent She was the subject of tax liens from both the IRS and the State of California totaling more than $450,000 in overdue taxes for the year of 2017. According to court documents, the artist was also the subject of overdue tax liens from 2018 totaling more than $780,000. Court records indicate she filed for bankruptcy in 1998 and 2010. The Wall Street Journal stated in 2011 that she reported $1.6 million in assets and $18.3 million in debts. That’s a lot of debt. It might be safe to say that managing money isn’t her strong suit. Nicolas Cage Between 1996 and 2011, he earned over $150 million and was one of Hollywood's highest-paid actors. Unfortunately, his wild spending habits got worse as his money grew. Cage claimed his financial adviser had led him "on a path toward financial ruin." Cage reportedly spent at least $33 million on rare art, pricey jewelry, dozens of specialty or historic automobiles, four luxury ships, and 15 luxurious properties, including two European castles and a deserted island in the Bahamas. In 2009, he was hit with a lien over $6 million for the delinquent taxes, interest and penalties. He eventually faced foreclosure on several properties and owed the IRS $12.9 million in property taxes. He managed to pay off $6.2 million in 2012 and continues to take film roles to pay off the rest of his debt. Judy Garland We all know her as Dorothy in The Wizard of Oz. She earned several acting awards over the span of her career, but sadly struggled with substance abuse and financial difficulties. Garland's tax debt in New York totaled $4 million in 1964. The IRS ended up foreclosing on her home, forcing her to spend years traveling from hotel to hotel before eventually dying of an accidental drug overdose in 1969. Many people probably didn’t know this about her because the internet and social media didn’t exist back then. If you’ve reached this far in the video, we would like to give a huge thank you! You can extend your support by subscribing to the channel and turning on that notification bell so you don’t miss any of the content we upload! Comment down below “I subbed” for your chance to be entered into our monthly shoutout giveaway! Ja Rule Now onto Ja Rule. In March 2011, the rapper plead guilty to submitting false tax returns and agreed to pay over $1 million in back taxes and penalties. He admitted he did not file his taxes for five years and agreed to pay the government more than $1.1 million in tax obligations and penalties imposed by the IRS. During sentencing, the judge said, “Taxpayers do not have the luxury of deciding whether to comply with laws.” He was sentenced to 28 months in prison and further sentenced to a year of supervised release. Stephen Baldwin For three years, between 2008 and 2010, The Usual Suspects star neglected to submit his income tax returns and make payments. He claimed his tax avoidance was not deliberate but, “Unfortunately, I got some really bad suggestions and advice ... from lawyers and accountants.” A plea bargain was reached to allow the actor’s record to be wiped clean if he paid back $300,000 within a year, and failure to pay that within that timeframe would result in a five-year sentence of probation where he would be expected to pay the full amount and follow certain conditions under supervision. Lauryn Hill According to the prosecution, the Grammy award-winning singer-rapper neglected to declare $2.3 million in earnings over a five-year period and owed the IRS nearly $1 million. In a statement to the judge, Hill said she had meant to pay the taxes but was unable to do so after withdrawing from public life and ending her music career to raise her children. She has six children, five of which she had with Bob Marley’s son Rohan Marley. According to documents presented during her trial, while earning almost $2 million between 2005 and 2007, she did not pay taxes. She ended up pleading guilty to the charges, but fought the sentencing, asking for leniency on the grounds of her charitable work, family circumstances, and the fact that she was estranged from the music industry. Her initial one-year sentence was then cut to three months in 2013 after she settled her state and federal taxes and penalties by paying back $970,000 she owed. She served her sentence at a minimum security prison where she was expected to work in the kitchen and gardens, and sleep in a dormitory. After leaving prison, she remained under home confinement for 3 additional months, followed by nine months of parole supervision. Fat Joe The What’s Luv rapper was initially charged with failing to submit income tax returns on more than $3.3 million of income between 2007 and 2010. However, in 2013, he pled guilty and accepted a plea deal so charges were dropped for failing to file for two of the four years. The rapper was granted leniency by the judge in exchange for his charitable works, which included giving computers to nearby schools, and for his payment of $718,000 in unpaid taxes prior to his sentence. He ended up receiving a four-month sentence and a $15,000 fine, followed by a one-year supervised release. Marc Anthony In 2007, the singer-songwriter was ordered to pay $2.5 million in back taxes to federal and state governments after failing to file his income tax returns on nearly $15.5 million between 2000 to 2004. He claimed it was the fault of his business management company. According to an inquiry by the Manhattan District Attorney's office, two of his managers and three of his corporate entities plead guilty to various tax crimes and offenses, including failing to submit corporate tax reports to New York State and City. Anthony's tax forms were prepared by a certified public accountant, and Marc presumably believed that the returns had been submitted and that any owed taxes had been paid. As a result, the office decided not to pursue charges against Anthony. You know what they say, “Mo money, mo problems.” And in the case of these celebrities, this was absolutely true. If you’ve learned anything from this video, we hope you’ve learned that neglecting to file your taxes and neglecting to pay your taxes are serious offenses! And if you’re going to take advice from someone, please make sure they know what they’re talking about. Which celebrities were you most surprised to learn about? - The Biggest Lies They Showed On MTV Cribs: Kind: captions Language: en Back in the 2000s, MTV Cribs was one of the former music video channel's most popular and fascinating shows. Each episode took viewers into the homes of pop stars, rappers, athletes, and actors, and the celebrities themselves gave guided tours of their luxurious surroundings. Of course, it was pretty much just a set up for them to flaunt their wealth. But if viewers ever felt envious of all that stuff those stars had, well, the stars might have felt envious, too. That's because many episodes of the show were staged… and actually full of lies. Here are some episodes of Cribs that weren't exactly truthful, to say the least. Robbie Williams Already a huge pop star in the U.K., the music industry tried its hardest to make Robbie Williams a big deal in the U.S., and he was forced into the spotlight just long enough to make an appearance on Cribs. But when the time came to shoot the episode and show off all his stuff, he borrowed a house and everything inside it. According to The Telegraph, the house really belonged to Jane Seymour. So yes, Robbie was guiding us all on a tour through the home of Dr. Quinn: Medicine Woman. JoJo A teen pop starlet in the 2000s, JoJo was just 13 when her song "Leave (Get Out)" became a hit in 2004, and a hit song meant it was time for an appearance on Cribs. However, the house viewers saw wasn't her house, she was actually kind of homeless at the time. JoJo told the Huffington Post that she was so busy touring, recording, and promoting her music that she and her mother were just living in hotels at the time. She added, "I should have rented out a place. But no. I just used my uncle’s crib. So that was me lying on Cribs." And after filming was done, she had to, well ..."Leave" and "(Get Out)." 50 Cent Curtis Jackson has won a bunch of awards. He's had a bunch of hit songs. But one thing he apparently didn't have, at least not when he appeared on Cribs, was a bunch of Ferraris. In 2007, Fiddy showed off his car collection on Cribs, which included three of the Italian stallions. But on a forum called Ferrari Chat, one user recalled asking 50 Cent about his rides at a car show just a couple of weeks before his Cribs episode, and the rapper said he owned five cars, none of which were Ferraris. Other eagle-eyed readers pointed out that all three cars were marked with the initials "SC," a notorious Ferrari collector who posted photos of the exact same cars as the ones that were supposed to be in 50 Cent's possession. "Yo, I'm part of the Ferrari club." Bow Wow When rapper Bow Wow boasted his way through his Miami mansion, he was especially proud of his fleet of very fancy cars. But while the movie Like Mike apparently made enough money to get Bow Wow a decent-sized home, these cars were not his. According to EGM Car Tech, car enthusiasts familiar with the Miami car scene noticed the word "Prestige" stamped on all three cars. That's the calling card for Prestige Luxury Rentals, a Miami car loan place, the perfect spot for all your reality show rental needs. Birdman Rapper and co-founder of Cash Money Records, Birdman was successful enough to get himself on MTV, and to get MTV to rent him a fancy car to show off on his Cribs episode. "I'ma bring you all to see how we pimp dat." Birdman was very proud to show viewers a maroon beast of a vehicle. But according to Uproxx, that beast was not his, it belonged to a Cincinnati car enthusiast. Ja Rule In 2001, rapper Ja Rule's handlers rented a gaudy, eight-bedroom Miami mansion with a waterfront view. In addition to letting the Cribs crew come to film, he also had a house-wrecker of a party, a violation of the rental agreement, for about 600 people. According to Contact Music, the owner of the house later sued Ja Rule and his associates, as their rental fee and deposit didn't quite cover all the damage and disgusting trash left behind. She also didn't actually give permission for Cribs to film in her house. Whoops. Redman Probably the greatest episode of Cribs was the one that completely subverted the concept of Cribs. Rapper Redman didn't lie, but MTV tried to get him to. The show visited his small Staten Island home — and it was kind of a mess. It was a refreshingly honest look into the real life of a star — which was supposed to be the entire point of the show. "They wanted me to rent a house so I could open the big double doors and s---, like 'yo look at my s---.'" "And my mother woulda told me, you know damn well that wasn't your g--damn house." He also said he knows a lot of other musicians who did lie for the show, but he won't name names. Thanks for watching! Click the Nicki Swift icon to subscribe to our YouTube channel. Plus check out all this cool stuff we know you'll love, too!

- Celebs Who Got Caught Telling Huge Lies: Kind: captions Language: en Some lies are worse than others. But when it comes to celebs, it's always a big story if they get caught fibbing. Whether it's an exaggeration about a relationship, or a cheating scheme that defrauded an entire sport for decades, these are the stars who got caught telling huge lies. Steve Rannazzisi Steve Rannazzisi old multiple interviewers that his entertainment career was inspired by a near lethal experience on September 11, 2001. The comedian and cast member on The League spun a tall tale, saying he was working in a Merrill Lynch office in the World Trade Center on the day of the attack. He obviously claimed to have escaped, but through the years, details about his 9/11 survival story changed. "I tweeted him saying, "Hey, I heard you got into comedy because of 9/11. I think that's really cool." And then he tweeted me back, 'Something like that.'" A New York Times piece outed him as a liar who gradually downplayed his story over the years. He went from claiming he was quote, "jostled all over the place" while working in the south tower when the first plane hit the north tower, to telling The Don Geronimo Show, "I was outside, really. I was working downtown at that time." "What were you doing in New York, were you a financial guy?" "No, I was just I was bouncing around." After the damning revelation, Rannazzisi apologized in a statement, and also tweeted: "For many years, more than anything, I have wished that, with silence, I could somehow erase a story told by an immature young man. It only made me more ashamed." A few weeks later, Rannazzisi further owned up to Howard Stern, chalking the lies up to it being his way of seeking acceptance from more famous comedians when he first moved to L.A. "Now I have to be the guy who's very strange and weird and just said I lied about 9/11." Brian Williams NBC News anchor Brian Williams told more than one doozy, often placing himself in the center of high-profile action. "I said things that weren't true.” According to the outlet, Williams stated that he was at Brandenburg Gate when the Berlin Wall came down, but later investigations revealed that he didn't arrive until the next day. "Our colleague Brian Williams is back in Kuwait City tonight after a close call in the skies over Iraq.” Williams also claimed a chopper he was riding in during the Iraq invasion was hit by an RPG. He amended his story to say his vehicle was behind the one that was hit, but sources suggested his chopper wasn't anywhere near the incident. He also claimed to have traveled into Baghdad with SEAL Team 6, which famously did away with Osama bin Laden. A spokesperson blew holes in that story, noting that journalists aren't embedded in counter-terrorism ops. Williams apologized for the chopper lie on NBC's Nightly News, saying, "I made a mistake in recalling the events of 12 years ago." Nevertheless, he was suspended in February 2015 for six months without pay, before finally being demoted to a position as a breaking-news anchor at MSNBC, per The Washington Post. Rebel Wilson Pitch Perfect star Rebel Wilson was exposed for lying about her age and her real name. In 2015, she was believed to be 29 - but that year, when she filed papers with the Australian Securities and Investment Commission, according to People, it was revealed that the funny gal was born in 1980 and was therefore actually 35 at the time. "Who is she?" "I am a legend... Rebelle." The Australian press also claimed she lied about her real name. Although she's claimed her parents legally named her "Rebel", she was actually born Melanie Elizabeth Bownds. She's also claimed to have a relatively rough upbringing - claims which the Australian magazine Woman's Day have called into question. "You are Australian. You're from Australia. Sydney? Yeah. Sydney. The western side which is the more gangsta side." After being called out, Wilson shot back at the press on Twitter with, "OMG I'm actually a 100 year old mermaid formerly known as 'CC Chalice' […] Thanks shady Australian press for your tall poppy syndrome." According to The Sydney Morning Herald, Wilson sued Bauer Media for defamation in 2017. "They maliciously took me down in May 2015 with a series of grubby and completely false articles." Her mother appeared in court, claiming that although her daughter had not been a private school-attending rich kid, the family had indeed lived in your average middle class suburb. Kylie Jenner Kylie Jenner's lips were one of the hottest topics of 2015. "I'm not ever gonna confirm or deny anything. It's too much." The youngest member of the Kardashian clan's inflated pout was much-discussed in the media - and she claimed she had achieved the look with just liner and lipstick. "I'm just not ready to talk to reporters about my lips yet because everyone always picks us apart." She finally came clean on a May 2015 episode of Keeping Up With The Kardashians when sister Khloe encouraged her to. In November 2016, Kylie explained to Complex why she was hesitant to tell the truth, saying, "I wasn't even 17 yet. What if I came out and said, 'Oh, yeah, I got my lips done'? […] I didn't want to be a bad influence. I didn't want people to think you had to get your lips done to feel good about yourself. […] I wish I had just been honest and upfront." Lance Armstrong "I'd probably do it again.” Cyclist Lance Armstrong was an international hero until a lie tarnished his legacy forever. His myth was perfect. He beat cancer - inspiring millions of people who also battled the disease - and his Livestrong culture let fans know that they, too, could defeat the odds. But an investigation revealed that the long-held rumor that Armstrong used performance-enhancing drugs to help win seven Tour de France titles was true. Armstrong vehemently denied the allegations for years, and even made a Nike commercial blasting the doping rumors, all while doping in real life. "I can emphatically say I'm not on drugs." Despite initial denials, he eventually admitted to doping in an interview with Oprah. "Did you ever take banned substances to enhance your cycling performance?" "Yes." He was subsequently stripped of his titles, and hit with a lifetime ban from competitive cycling in 2012. Victoria Beckham Former Spice Girl and beloved fashion designer Victoria Beckham's bust seemingly grew overnight, and to some, seemed too big to be natural for her very petite frame. "This haunts me like you wouldn't believe." For a long time, Beckham denied she underwent breast enhancement surgery, telling Piers Morgan in 2003, "I'm completely natural, except for my fingernails, and I have a bit of help with my hair and a bit of a St. Tropez [fake tan] going on." However, during a 2014 interview with Allure, while looking at older photos of herself with larger cleavage, Beckham said, "I don't have them anymore […] I think I may have purchased them." In 2017, she penned a letter to her 18-year-old self, for Vogue, writing, "Don't mess with your boobs. All those years I denied it - stupid. A sign of insecurity. Just celebrate what you've got." Brad Pitt & Angelina Jolie Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie's relationship was born on the set of Mr. & Mrs. Smith. The thing was, Brad was married to Jennifer Aniston while filming the movie. "So what do we do?" Brad and Angie maintained that their romance began after Brad and Jen had split. However, a year after the movie's release, Jolie eventually admitted to Vogue that she and Pitt indeed fell in love on set. "It took until, really, the end of [shooting Mr. & Mrs. Smith] for us, I think, to realize that it might mean something more than we'd earlier allowed ourselves to believe. And both knowing that the reality of that was a big thing, something that was going to take a lot of serious consideration." Aniston reacted in her own Vogue cover story, calling Jolie's statement, quote, "uncool" and "inappropriate," since Aniston was completely unaware of the budding romance between her husband and his leading lady. It also seemed like an admission on Jolie's part that things had indeed started up while Pitt was still married to Aniston. Jack & Meg White When The White Stripes rose to fame in 2002, their charming brand of Detroit garage rock took over the airwaves. The band consisted of singer and guitarist Jack White and drummer Meg White, who were presented as being brother and sister. Except, they weren't. In reality, they were exes-— formerly married in 1996, and divorced by 2000. Jack explained their reasoning for the ruse to Rolling Stone in 2005, saying, "When you see a band that is two pieces, husband and wife, boyfriend and girlfriend, you think. 'Oh, I see […].' When they're brother and sister, you go, 'Oh, that's interesting.' You care more about the music, not the relationship - whether they're trying to save their relationship by being in a band. You don't think about that with a brother and sister. They're mated for life. That's what family is like." Jussie Smollett "Oh, it's not necessarily that you don't believe that this is the truth. You don't even want to see the truth." Empire star Jussie Smollett claimed he was brutally attacked by two white men wearing ski masks in Chicago around 2 a.m. on January 29, 2019. According to TMZ, one of the men shouted racist and anti-gay slurs before the two assailants fractured Smollett's rib, tied a rope around his neck, and poured a liquid on him that was possibly bleach. In an interview with Chicago police, Smollett claimed the attackers yelled, quote, "This is MAGA country," a reference to President Trump's slogan, "Make America Great Again." The Chicago P.D. said it was investigating the assault as a possible hate crime. But shortly after, reports that Smollett had staged the attack began to surface. A little more than two weeks after the alleged incident, Smollett was arrested and charged with filing a false police report. After the arrest, Chicago Police Superintendent Eddie Johnson condemned Smollett's alleged behavior in a blistering press conference, asking, quote, "Why would anyone, especially an African-American man, use the symbolism of a noose to make false accusations?" "How can an individual who's been embraced by the city of Chicago turn around and slap everyone in this city in the face by making these false claims?" In an even more bizarre twist, prosecutors later dropped all of the charges after a secret deal was struck with Smollett. The Chicago Tribune then reported: "The entire court file has been sealed at the defense’s request. […] The unanswered questions surrounding the development left many wondering what might have happened behind the scenes." Miley Cyrus In a January 2013 interview with Cosmo, former Hannah Montana star Miley Cyrus referred to Liam Hemsworth as her quote, "hubby" and said, "I’m happy I don’t have to date anyone. […] I was like, ‘I’m so happy I’m married, because you guys are cruel!'" That led many fans to believe that the pair had secretly wed. Hemsworth's rep quickly denied the news to People magazine. Since then, the couple has had their up and downs, including a brief split in 2013 after Hemsworth was spotted with actress Eiza Gonzalez and Cyrus was caught getting cozy with Arnold Schwarzenegger's son, Patrick. The pair eventually reconciled in 2015 and officially tied the knot in a secret ceremony in 2018. In their first appearance as husband and wife, Hemsworth explained how they kept the news of their wedding so quiet, admitting to Extra, "No It wasn't hard" "It was mainly immediate family, a spur-of-the-moment kind of thing. I am very happy to be where I am." Check out one of our newest videos right here! Plus, even more Nicki Swift videos about your favorite stars are coming soon. Subscribe to our YouTube channel and hit the bell so you don't miss a single one.

that's refreshingly ordinary get ready to chuckle as we explore the quirky choices of Hollywood's most relatable stars who live like they are poor Keanu Reeves we have all had dreams of how we would live if we ever get rich beyond our imagination but here's Keanu Reeves who has all the riches and still lives a regular life Keanu Reeves the Canadian actor known for his roles in iconic films like the Matrix and John Wick has an estimated net worth of $380 million as of 2024 his wealth primarily stems from a successful acting career that began in the 1980s with notable films such as Bill and Ted's Excellent Adventure speed and Point Break however it was The Matrix trilogy that catapulted him to super stardom with earnings from the franchise exceeding $200 million across its installments relocate frequently for work he faced instability that contributed to his grounded nature he has endured profound personal losses including the death of his close friend River Phoenix from a drug overdose and the tragic loss of his partner Jennifer Sim who died in a car accident after suffering a miscarriage these experiences shaped his world viw fostering a sense of empathy and humility he has often expressed that such tragedies have made him value life and the people in it more deeply steering him away from a flashy lifestyle despite his immense fortune and tragic moments Reeves is famously known for his modest lifestyle he resides in a relatively simple apartment in Los Angeles rather than a sprawling Mansion which is a stark contrast to many of his Hollywood peers this Choice Though he frequently uses his platform to educate others about this illness, the physical pain and health anxieties are always present, transforming a vibrant star into someone who must struggle with his body's limitations. Though the disease has severely impacted her life and career, she continues to thrive in her creative endeavors, becoming a symbol of resilience. The battle with MS is an arduous journey, but she bravely confronted it and transformed her pain into motivation, becoming an inspiration for others in similar circumstances. Lena Dunham. Lena Dunham, the talented writer, actress, and director, has been outspoken about living with endometriosis and Aaylor's Danlos syndrome, a series of conditions causing chronic pain and incessant difficulties in life. She has highlighted issues of chronic pain and women's health, unmasking the horrific burdens many endure. Her story is a heart-rening testament to the cruelties of illness, transforming a talented young woman into a ceaseless warrior fighting physical and mental pain. - Hollywood Actress Buys Town, Goes Bankrupt: Kind: captions Language: en In the late 1980s to early 1990s, Kim Basinger was pretty much unstoppable. She was one of the most recognizable faces in Hollywood, headlining blockbusters, hitting magazine covers, and pulling in millions of dollars. She had money, fame, and in this moment, right in the middle of her rise to the top of stardom, she did something kind of strange. She bought a town. Kim didn't buy a mansion. She didn't buy a big ranch like a lot of celebrities did. She literally bought an entire town. The town was called Brassleton, Georgia. It came with 1,751 acres, and what she planned to do with that town was about as ambitious as you can get. Kim wanted to build a creative empire in the middle of rural Georgia with film studios, music festivals. She wanted to create a haven for artists and maybe, just maybe, she would make a ton of money in the process. Ever since Kim Basinger bought Brazleton, people have been anxious to see what would happen next and just when their high-profile land lady might come to town. Well, they got that visit today, but it looks like the answers about Brazleton's future will still be a while in coming. What started as a dream turned into one of the most bizarre and expensive missteps in celebrity real estate history. It involved bankruptcy, lawsuits, some angry locals, and years later, it would quietly help inspire one of the funniest TV shows of the last decade. This is the story of how Kim Basinger bought a town in the 1990s and the unexpected chain of events that followed. Okay, so let's rewind the clock a little bit. It's the late 1980s and Kim Basinger is becoming one of the most known women in Hollywood. She starts out as a model in New York. She gets her big break in television and then by the time she's in her early 30s, she's a full-blown movie star. We're talking roles at a bunch of movies, including Batman, which was at the time one of the biggest box office hits of the decade. At this point, Kim Basinger isn't just famous, she's like famous famous. She's making millions. Her name's everywhere. She's in the tabloids. And like a lot of celebrities who suddenly come into huge wealth, Kim starts to look for ways to invest. You know, you've got the usual stuff, houses, endorsements, luxury, everything. But she wants to do something a little bit different, something that ties her back to her roots. See, Kim's from Georgia originally. She's born and raised in Athens. So, when she starts looking around for investment ideas, she's naturally drawn back home. It was at this point when someone pitches her the idea to buy a town, like not just a piece of land, an entire town. Like I said, with Kim having roots in Georgia, naturally, this town was located in Georgia. The town was called Brassleton. It's just a tiny little dot on the map, about 50 mi northeast of Atlanta. When I say tiny, I mean tiny. Back in 1989, the population was hovering around 500 people. But it did have everything you'd expect to see in an old town. Brassleton had a bank, a small industrial park, a handful of mom and pop shops, a post office. Kim didn't see this as just some sleepy old town. She saw potential here. At the time, Brassleton was largely undeveloped. It had a lot of land, plenty of highway frontage, and just enough infrastructure to make development possible. It wasn't thriving, but it also wasn't exactly dead either. It looked like a town that had a ton of potential, and to the right person, it kind of looked like a blank canvas. The deal itself was massive. Kim and a small group of investors, including some family members, purchased roughly 1751 acres of land in and around Brassleton. And they paid a whopping $20 million for it. An incredible amount of money, especially for the 1980s. The vision here seemed pretty clear at the start. Kim wasn't trying to just buy herself a quirky vanity project. This was, at least in her mind at the time, a real business play. Many of Brazleton's 500 residents have been waiting a long time for Kim to come and tell them what she plans to do with the town she bought more than a year ago. Something the Wall Street Journal wondered too in a recent article. She talked openly about wanting to bring jobs to the area. It would be a town that could attract musicians, filmmakers, writers, creatives of all kinds. There was talks about opening up a production studio, a film school, maybe even a theme park. It wasn't exactly all mapped out yet, but the vibe was clear here. like this was supposed to be a new kind of town that was built around creativity. In interviews at the time, Kim sounded passionate. You could tell that this wasn't just some investment for her. She saw it as a legacy move, something that could give back to her home state and then also be profitable at the same time. But this is where the wheels start to wobble a little bit because having passion and vision is nice, but let's be honest, passion doesn't build towns. money does and so does infrastructure and planning and experience. And unfortunately, let's face it, Kim didn't have much of that. I mean, she was an actress and acting was definitely her area of expertise. It became pretty quickly apparent that buying Brassleton was the easy part, but figuring out what to actually do with it, that's where things started to unravel. From all accounts that I could find, there wasn't even a detailed business plan. like Kim had some ideas, but there was no timeline, no road map, and no real team in place to execute that plan that didn't even exist. Another thing too that's interesting to remember is that this was a time that was before the internet. So, back then, you had to do things the old-fashioned way, and you had to get out there and network with people and meet investors and pitch your plans and brainstorm with a bunch of other like-minded individuals, which was a lot harder to do back then than it is nowadays. Anyways, Brassleton locals weren't exactly thrilled by what was happening or what wasn't happening. A lot of them were actually skeptical. They liked their quiet town. They weren't looking for it to turn into some Hollywood playground. Everybody seems to be kind of disappointed right now because they thought when she bought this thing, they'd do something with it right away. I reckon with no visible progress, no construction, no job creation, nothing concrete, the public started to get a bit sketched out by the whole ordeal. Fast forward to 1993, and this is where things started to go south. Kim was working on a big movie at the time called Boxing Helena, which if you never heard of that movie before, that's fine. I hadn't either. Apparently, it's a movie about how a surgeon becomes obsessed with some girl and then amputates both of her legs. Seems super weird. Kim also must have thought that this movie concept was weird because she actually signed up to do the movie, but she later pulled out after the funds were already committed, which the producers were not happy about at all. Basically, this movie was banking on her to be the lead actress. It probably wouldn't have even been made at all without her. But the movie did end up getting made and it went on to completely flop at the box office presumably because Kim wasn't in it. Like I said, the producers were furious. They sued and they won. The court hit Kim with an 8.9 million judgment. Combine that with the money that she had sunk into Brassleton and suddenly Kim Basinger was broke. She filed for bankruptcy in 1993, meaning Brassleton, it was basically worthless. By 1995, the dream of owning a town in Georgia was officially 100% dead. Kim sold her interest in Brassleton. The exact amount that Kim got for her shares isn't clear, but the buyer was a developer named Wayne Mason. And unlike Kim, Mason actually had a development team. He had a plan. Over the next couple of decades, Brassleton slowly transformed. Today, the city is a pretty normal suburban community with about 13,000 residents. It's got a winery, a resort, some shopping, and a growing population. Seems like a pretty nice place to live, to be honest. But there's definitely no film studio, no cultural mecca. And if you walk through downtown Brassleton today, you won't find a trace of the actress who once owned the whole thing. Years later, after Kim Basinger's dream in Brassleton fell apart, it did actually go on to inspire the TV show Shitz Creek. Anybody who's seen the show probably would recognize the similarities. We got a wealthy celebrity who buys a small town but then loses everything. Shitz Creek isn't a biography or anything, but the bones are definitely there. And what's cool is that Kim watched the show and apparently when she found out about the connection, she said it was hilarious, which I agree with her. It was a good show. In a way, the Shitz Creek inspiration makes for the most Hollywood ending of all. We had a massive personal failure which got turned into a hit TV show. Kind of a full circle moment. Kim Basinger's story is a fascinating story of ambition. Obviously, it's a pretty sad story, too. Although, I will say that these events didn't completely ruin her career. She continued acting and she even went on to win an Academy Award for LA Confidential in 1998. It's definitely one of the more bizarre real estate ventures that we've ever seen a celebrity take on, but I got to say, hats off to Kim for at least giving it a try. I'll see you guys next week.

एंड अनुष्का होम इट इज सच अ प्लीजिंग अंडररेटेड कलर होम टीरियस एंड सो स्मार्टली यूज्ड नाउ एन अनयूजुअल एग्जांपल ऑफ अ स्टेटमेंट पीस वड बी दिस डबल हाइट स्टोन सडन वल विराट होंग नेन इट्स सच अ सटल अटेम्प्ट टू डॉ पर्पस आई मीन लुक एट दैट इज इट गॉर्जियस द थर्ड लिमिट इज अ कन्नल सेंट्रिक स्पेस स व्ट इज दैट दज आर द स्पेसेस वेर द फर्निचर इज अरेंज इन अ मैनर दैट एंकरेजेस पीपर टू इंटरेक्ट विथ ईच अदर एज एन आर्किटेक्ट आईम ऑलवेज इंक्लाइंड टुवर्ड्स आइडिया ऑफ हैविंग कॉ ल सीटेल एंड दिस इज अ ह्यूज थम्स अप फॉर मी सो फॉर एग्जांपल आई लाइक द कॉन्कशन पिक दैट इज इन सुनाक्षी सिलास बालकनी इट्स परफेक्ट फॉर दोज इवनिंग कॉफीस एंड सुपर लेजी सन बंगस आल्सो व्हाट आई लव अबाउट विराट कोरी होम इज दैट हिज लिविंग रूम सीटिंग इज अरेंज बडि स्पाइट द डाइनिंग टेबल फेसिंग ईच अदर द रूम हैज नो टेलीविजन सो द रूम इज पोरली मेंट फॉर फैमिली कन्वरसेशंस फूड एंड लस्ट नाउ द फोर्थ एलिमेंट इज लैंडस्केप ंग व्हिच इ एक्सट्रीमली इंपोर्टेंट फॉर स्पेसेस लाइक बालकनीज डेक्स पैटू और टेरेसेज व्हाट दे डू इज दे हेल्प ब्रिंग द आउटडोर्स इनटू योर होम दे इंप्रूव द एयर क्वालिटी एंड दे आल्सो इंप्रूव योर हेल्थ नाउ वेदर यू लुक एट मिथिला पार्कर्स होम सुनाक्षी होम और इवन जावेद जाफरीज होम ऑल ऑफ देम हैव ब्यूटीफुल प्लांट इनडोर्स एंड इवल इफ यू लुक एट दी स्मॉल प्लांटर पॉकेट्स इन विराट कोलीज लिविंग रूम दे क्रिएट अ विजुअल डिफरेंस इंड एड नेचुरल टेक्सचर टू द ओवरऑल डिजाइन सो द फिफ्थ एलिमेंट इज स्मार्ट इंटीरियर सॉल्यूशंस एंड नाउ दिस इज यरी इंटरेस्टिंग द डिजइन एलिमेंट्स दैट एन हैंस द फंक्शनालिस मार्ट इंटीरियर सॉल्यूशंस सो लेट्स कंसीडर सुनाक्षी सीनस एज एन एग्जांपल दिस मर्फी बेड इज सच अ कोर फीचर दैट एक्ट्स एज अ स्पेस सेमी एलमेंट इन द ल एंड इफ यू आल्सो लुक एट दिस सीमलेस डोर इन वन ऑफ़ द रूम्स इन मदुल एंड अदि त्यास होम द डिज़ाइन ऑफ़ द डोर ब्लड्स विद द वॉल पैनलिंग टू क्रिएट अ हारमोनियसली सो अनदर गुड एग्जांपल फ्रॉम देर होम आर दीज पैनल्स दैट कन स्पी द टीमिंग यूनिट बिहाइंड देम आई थिंक इट्स अ विन सिचुएशन सो द लिविंग रूम कैन बी अ कन्वर्सेशन स्पेस बाय द डे एंड द मूवी थिएटर बाय द लाइट डिपेंडिंग ऑन हाउ दे वांट इड टू बी द सिक्स्थ एलिमेंट इज अ यूनिक सरफेस फिनिश नाउ दिस इज अ मटेरियल दैट अलाउ यू टू पर्सनलाइज योर स्पेस योर वे वेदर इट इज अ नेचुरल स्टोन वॉलपेपर विनियर और टेक्सचर पेंट अ यूनिक सरफेस फिनिश एड्स कैरेक्टर टू द स्टोरी ऑफ योर होम फॉर एग्जांपल लुक एट दिस इंडिया इंस्पायर्ड वॉलपेपर इन सोनम कपूर्स होम शी वांट टू ब्रिंग द एसेंस ऑफ हर होमलैंड टू हर को इन लंडन एंड इट इज सो ब्यूटीफुली कैप्चरड हियर आल्सो दिस मैक वड लैमिनेट ऑल अक्रॉस द वल्स इन बदुल एंड अदद को रिफ्लेक्ट द वाथ ऑफ देर पर्सनालिटी इन द बेस ऑफ रेजिडेंस एंड नाउ दैट वी नो व्हाट मेक्स अ सेलेब्रिटी होम सो फैबुलस लेट्स डिकोड हाउ वी कैन ब्रिंग अ सिमिलर चार्म टू आवर होमस नंबर वन मेक प्रोविजन फॉर एपल नेचुरल लाइट एंड वेंटिलेशन इन योर गो फॉर लार्जर विंडोज इन नॉर्थ एंड नॉर्थ वेस्ट डायरेक्शन एज रिसीव डिफ्यूज लाइट बट इफ योर अपार्टमेंट हैज अ साउथ एंड साउथ ईस्ट डायरेक्शन फेसिंग डायरेक्ट हार्श सलाइट यू कैन इंस्टॉल ग्लेज्ड विंडो ग्लासेस टू रिड्यूस हीट आल्सो इफ पॉसिबल यू विन कवर द ओपनिंग विद द लवर्स टू क्रिएट शेड एंड डिफ्यूज द लाइट एंटरिंग योर होम नंबर टू फाइंड द स्टेटमेंट पीज फॉर योर वूल दैट डिपिक्ट्स योर पोनर टिपिकली पीपल चूज वसेस लैंप्स एक्सेंट चेयर्स मिरर एंड स्कल्पचर्स फॉर दिस पर्पस नाउ म पीपल थिंक दैट द स्टेटमेंट पीस शुड बी बोल्ड और एक्स्ट्रा वेगें बट आई थिंक इट कैन आल्सो बी अ सटल एडिशन लाइक व सेडिंग मटेरियल और इवन फ्लोरिंग पैटर्न फॉर दैट मैटर सिपली सीकिंग हेयर डू वास चूज समथिंग दैट इज ट्रू टू योर पर्सनालिटी नंबर थ्री क्रिएट स्पेसस फॉर कन्वर्सेशन वेदर इट्स होम्स और ऑफिसेज र सो मच एक्सपोजर टू सींस दैट इट इज डिफिकल्ट टू अवॉइड देन सो मेक श्यर दैट यू हैव अ डेडिकेटेड स्पेस इन योर होम व्हिच इज लाइक अ नो डिवाइस टोन इट इज योर प्लेस टू सिट फ योर फैमिली रिलैक्स एंड जस्ट सेलिब्रेट लाइव्स यू कैन ब्रिंग इन अ लॉट ऑफ मूवेबल फर्नीचर आइटम्स लाइक ऑटोमन टू ऐड सीटिंग टू योर बफे टेबल एंड ट्रांसफॉर्म योर लिविंग रूम फ दिस एग्जैक्ट पर्पस नंबर फोर ब्रिंग प्लांट्स इन टू योर बोम एंड प्लीज डू नॉट थिंक अबाउट यूजिंग आर्टिफिशियल वंस द फ्रेशनेस एंड पॉजिटिविटी ऑफ़ लाइ प्लांट्स कैन नॉट एवर बी रिप्लेस बाय प्लास्टिक क्रीन सो डू अ लिटिल वप अबाउट व्हाट आर द काइंड ऑफ़ प्लांट्स दैट कैन थ्राइज इन योर क्लाइमेट इफ यू आर अ बिगनर आई वुड रिकमेंड गो विथ पीसीडी एंड डी प्लांट्स एज दे आर योर मेंटेन एंड इफ यू हैव अ बिग इनफ बैगनी इन्वेस्ट इन सम गुड क्वालिटी आउ दर फर्निचर एंड ट्रस्ट मी यू विल थैंक मी लेटर आई हैव रीस ली एडेड पेबर चेर सेटप इन माय बालकनी एंड नाउ इट इज माय फेवरेट स्पॉट टू सिप माय कॉफी बेस्ड ऑन आइडियाज और इवन जस्ट एंजॉय द सनसेट नंबर फाइव डिजइन स्पेसेस इन योर मम टू बी मल्टी पर्पस यूज स्पेसिफिक फर्निचर लाइक फोल्डेबल टेबल्स मर्फी बेड और पुल्ड आउन स्टोरेजेस टू ऑप्टिमाइज द प्लानिंग ऑफ योर फोम आल्सो इफ यू हैव द वैन विड्थ यू कैन कंसीडर इनकॉरपोरेटिंग मबल वॉल्स टू ट्रांजिशन योर फ इनटू योर पर्सनलाइज्ड हेवन दिस विल हेल्प यू मेक द मोस्ट आट ऑफ योर स्पेस बाइल गिविंग द अपॉर्चुनिटी टू एक्सपेरिमेंट विद द एस्थेटिक्स ऑफ द स्पेस नंबर सिक्स योर वॉल शुड टेल योर स्टोरी सो इवोक द क्रिएटिविटी विद इन यू एंड फाइंड अ मटेरियल और डिजाइन एलिमेंट व्हिट अलाउ यू टू एक्सप्रेस योर नैरेटिव कस्टमाइज वॉलपेपर्स आर एन एक्सलेंट वे टू डू दैट अदर वाइज यू कैन आल्सो यूज स्टोट फिनिशेज सेरामिक टाइज वियर्स लैमिनेट्स और इवन पीवीसी पैनल्स टू कस्टमाइज द स्पेस टू योर लाइकिंग सो टू सम इट अप द कोर इंस्पिरेशन दैट ड्राइव सेलेब्रिटी होम डिजाइन इज सेलिब्रेटिंग द लिटिल थिंग्स इन लाइफ सो गो अहेड एंड मेक स्पेसेस दैट हेल्प यू अचीव दैट नाउ इफ यू आर एक्साइटेड टू डिजाइन योर होम लाइक योर फेवरेट सेलिब्रिटीज आई वांट टू नो लेट मी नो इन द कमेंट्स बिलो एंड इफ यू हैव एनी क्वेश्चंस फील फ्री टू ड्रॉप देम इन द कमेंट्स एज वेल डोंट फॉरगेट टू लाइक एंड सब्सक्राइब इफ दिस वीडियो हेल्प

- How Do Celebrities Maintain Privacy in Their Homes? | Everything About LA: Kind: captions Language: en How do celebrities maintain privacy in their homes? Imagine living in a world where every moment of your life is under constant scrutiny. For many celebrities, this is a daily reality. However, they have developed various strategies to carve out a semblance of privacy within their homes. One of the most effective methods is the use of high-tech security systems. These systems often include advanced surveillance cameras, motion detectors, and alarm systems that provide a robust layer of protection against intrusions. Many celebrities invest significantly in these technologies, ensuring that their homes are equipped with the latest innovations in security. In addition to technology, physical barriers play a crucial role in maintaining privacy. High walls, gated entrances, and strategically placed landscaping can create a secluded environment. Celebrities often choose properties that are situated on large lots or in exclusive neighborhoods where access is restricted. This not only deters unwanted visitors, but also provides a sense of safety and tranquility. Another common practice is hiring professional security personnel. Many celebrities employ private security teams to monitor their properties and provide aroundthe-clock protection. These professionals are trained to handle various situations, ensuring that their clients can enjoy their homes without fear of intrusion. Additionally, some celebrities may choose to live in homes that are designed with privacy in mind, featuring layouts that minimize visibility from the outside. Social media also presents a challenge to privacy. To combat this, many celebrities are selective about what they share online. They may choose to keep their personal lives private, sharing only curated glimpses of their lives with their followers. This strategic approach helps to maintain a boundary between their public personas and private lives. Furthermore, some celebrities utilize non-disclosure agreements with staff and visitors to ensure that their personal information remains confidential. By legally binding individuals to privacy agreements, they can feel more secure in their interactions within their homes. Ultimately, maintaining privacy in their homes requires a combination of technology, physical barriers, professional security, and personal discretion. By employing these strategies, celebrities can create a sanctuary where they can relax and enjoy their lives away from the public.

- 10 Privacy & Safety Tips for Influencers: Kind: captions Language: en - There were so many mistakes that my wife and I made when we started creating content on social media over 10 years ago. When you're putting yourself out there, a lot of times you're so focused on making great content that you don't consider how critical it is to keep yourself safe especially if you have a family or young kids. Look, the internet is a great place to connect with people all around the world and make a career doing something you love but it can also have its downsides. There are trolls, scammers, and just all around dishonest people who will try to figure out ways to take advantage of you and even your loved ones. I'm not trying to be scary, trust me. I just want everyone out there to be safe. So here are 10 must do safety and privacy tips for influencers. First up, be cognizant of your surroundings when you're filming, don't show the outside front of your home especially your street number. And that includes not tagging locations too close to your home. If you're filming in your car, follow a similar practice of not showing too much, especially if you might keep something in there expensive, like camera equipment. And keep street signs out of you. A good rule of thumb is just to shoot at a high angle pointing down. When it comes to filming inside your house, be mindful of any packages or mail that show your address and try to avoid windows that reveal too much about your surrounding location as well. And speaking of locations, the second tip I have for you is if you have kids, don't take pictures or don't film at your kids' school. This should be a hard rule, not only for the safety of your kids, but also for the safety of other kids who attend that school. Be conscious of showing other children or their friends in any setting. The rule of thumb is ask permission from their parents first. In fact, in general, just be respectful of your friends and your family and ask them if they want to be filmed or tagged on social media before you do it. Tip number three, get a UPS Store Box, not a P.O. Box, for a couple of reasons. Number one, you won't have to give out your personal address. Number two, some brands can't ship things to you in a P.O. Box like perishable items. And number three, if, and when you decide to formalize your creator business as an LLC or an S Corp you can use your UPS Store address so that your home address doesn't show up on the the Secretary of State's website where things like addresses are easy to access by the public. Now we're going to get a little mission impossible here on this next one, but for good reasons. Number four is get a security system. An alarm is great, but more importantly is a camera system. You can purchase affordable and very effective door and security cameras that are easy to place around areas of your home. Some of these even allow you to monitor through your phone. So you could be out of Starbucks a few miles away and actually see if there's any sneaky activity going on around your home. Of course, we can't talk about homes safety without talking about your little ones. So tip number five is talk to your kids about strangers. Believe it or not, sometimes I think we just assume that our kids know better. You need to make it a habit to keep your kids prepared to know exactly what to do if approached by a stranger. For starters, communicate to them that if someone they've never met before ever approaches them, don't listen to anything they say unless you're there with them. Even if the stranger knows their name from you posting about it on social media. It's not okay to interact or go anywhere with this stranger without your permission. And since we're on the topic of kids, tell your kids' school or daycare about what you do. I know some people are kind of embarrassed that they create content online, but what's worse? A little embarrassment or something happening to your kid. Maybe as informative as you can so that they can understand that it's important to never release any information to anyone without your direct approval. And most importantly, never allow your child to leave with anyone. You specifically have an approved of first. Tip number seven, add two-step authentication on all your social media. Yes, all your social media. I know, I know you have the best and most amazing password ever, but still, I highly recommend to not use text message-based authentication, but instead use an authenticator app like Google authenticator. These apps make it much harder for people to hack into your social media. Remember, if you make a significant portion of your livelihood from social media, you need to take how well you're securing access to those accounts really seriously. You never wanna get into a situation where you get hacked and your entire career that you've spent years building is just gone like that. Tip number eight is to also add verbal passwords on important accounts like your bank. This is going to be monumental in preventing identity theft. Many influencers have a lot of information about them floating around out there. Their name, where they live, names of all their family members, their birthdays, where they were born and so on. So you can start to see how easily it might be for someone with bad intentions to piece together all this information and try to mess with your life. Having a verbal password could be the last line of defense to preventing your identity from being stolen and almost impossible for thieves to get a hold of it. As long as you don't use something obvious as your verbal password. So we've been hammering home about safety and privacy in well, in your home. But what about when you're not at home? Let's talk about travel. So tip number nine, if possible, don't post on social media when you're traveling unless you have to for a sponsored opportunity. In fact, it's best to not post anything until you get home. This does two things. First, it prevents burglars from targeting your home while you're gone. If you don't post anything until you get back, they basically missed any real opportunity to take advantage. Second, this is gonna make it hard for people to find you particularly stalkers. Even if you don't have any stalkers per se, you still wanna be able to go out and enjoy your travels without followers tracking you down. And tip number 10. If you do meet ups where you meet your fans or your followers consider not bring your kids. If you do bring your kids, consider hiring security. The chances of something bad happening are probably pretty low, but having security presence, it's gonna reduce the risk and your stress by a considerable amount. So you might be thinking, "Okay, I get it. You need to be careful, but this stuff doesn't really apply to me. I don't have that big of a following so I don't have stalkers or people trying to find out a bunch of information about me." Okay, fine but what about in three years when you blow up? Basically no one deletes anything that they post on the internet. So you have to assume that anything you post between now and when you blow up, will be there forever. So I always recommend to creators to just start being as careful as possible now, so that you're able to protect yourself and your family so that you never have to deal with any of this kind of stuff. So let me know in the comments, any safety or privacy tips that you've picked up along the way. Hit subscribe and click the notification bell so you'll know when my next video comes out. And watch this video right here, come on. You know you want to, come on.

- What Rules Do Celebrities Set For Staff Discretion In Their Homes? - The Rich And Famous Files: Kind: captions Language: en [Music] What rules do celebrities set for staff discretion in their homes? Imagine living in a world where your every move is watched and your privacy is a precious gift. Celebrities face this everyday and their household staff play a big role in keeping their lives private. So what exactly do they tell their staff about sharing information? First off, staff must keep all private details under lock and key. This means no sharing about the celebrity's personal lives, health, schedules, or family matters. Many celebrities use formal agreements called non-disclosure agreements to make sure everyone understands the importance of secrecy. If a staff member breaks this rule, there are serious legal consequences. Staff are also trained to be very careful about what they say and to whom. They learn to speak only when necessary and to keep conversations about sensitive topics private. This helps prevent accidental leaks or gossip. Respecting personal boundaries is another key rule. Staff are expected to focus only on their duties and avoid getting involved in the family's personal or emotional issues. They are there to serve, not to become part of the family drama. Gossiping or talking to the media is strictly forbidden. Celebrities want to avoid any chance of their lives being splashed across newspapers or social media. Staff are told not to discuss their employers online or with outsiders. They are also expected to be proactive and discreet. This means anticipating the family's needs quietly and efficiently, so there are no disruptions or surprises. Security is a big concern. So many celebrity homes have gatekeepers or security staff who check visitors, deliveries, and phone calls. Only trusted people are allowed in, which helps keep the family safe and private. When it comes to children, staff like nannies are given clear rules about respecting their privacy. They create a normal environment for the kids while shielding them from public attention. All these rules help celebrities live comfortably without constant intrusion. The combination of legal agreements, professional training, and clear expectations creates a safe space where their personal lives stay private. Staff discretion isn't just about trust. It's about protecting the family's peace and security in a world that's always watching.

Brian Lee (06:12): Law Garden was our earliest iteration of … sorry,&nbsp;&nbsp; it was me and Brian Lou, who I went to law school&nbsp; with and we were best friends, and our first idea&nbsp;&nbsp; was that we would have stay-at-home attorneys,&nbsp; answer questions online for $0.99 cents a minute.&nbsp;&nbsp; That was the general idea. Brian Lee (06:55): The second thing is that&nbsp;&nbsp; the malpractice insurance was going&nbsp; to be pretty high, but number three,&nbsp;&nbsp; at the end of the day, you’re still practicing&nbsp; law providing legal advice, and as such,&nbsp;&nbsp; we would be classified as a law firm and&nbsp; therefore cannot raise capital from non-attorneys.&nbsp;&nbsp; There’s a lot of fee-splitting rules in law.&nbsp; We realized that we couldn’t really start that&nbsp;&nbsp; because we couldn’t raise outside capital to get&nbsp; it done, we could never take the company public,&nbsp;&nbsp; and there were a lot of regulations involved with&nbsp; it, so we moved on and started Legalzoom instead. Brian Lee (08:44): That’s the environment, but we really&nbsp;&nbsp; believed in technology and what the internet held&nbsp; and the potential of it, and so without raising&nbsp;&nbsp; any capital, we quit our jobs and we just started&nbsp; working out of our condo and just building. Brian Lee (10:03): I’m not exactly sure, Trey. I think I was just&nbsp;&nbsp; born with a lot of grit.
